Explanation:
This report presents a well-structured analysis of chromatographic peak shape quality in biopharmaceutical manufacturing. Strengths include clear methodology organized in three logical steps, appropriate application of USP <621> standards, and relevant statistical techniques (Z-score outlier detection). The writing is professional and technical jargon is well-explained. 

However, several limitations reduce overall impact. The results section lacks quantitative findings—no specific numbers of outliers detected, failure percentages, or statistical summaries are reported, only general statements about successful application. The visual assessment (Figure 1) is present but lacks clarity regarding which peaks represent actual anomalies. The analysis could benefit from more detailed discussion of practical implications and deeper investigation into root causes of identified deviations. The code appendix demonstrates implementation competence but extends the document significantly. The methodology, while sound, is relatively standard in chromatography analysis. Additional statistical rigor (e.g., confidence intervals, comparison against control baselines, or validation metrics) would strengthen scientific credibility.
